# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "deno"; // 1
serve(async (req) => {              //2 & 3
    const url = new URL(req.url);   //4&5
    
    if (!['/api', '/load'].includes(url.pathname)) return;               //6         
     
    let modulePath: string | undefined  = (await Deno.readTextFile('./module_config'))?.trim();  //7 &8   Module configuration is loaded from a file on each request to avoid caching issues, or user can be configuring via environment variables/query parameters if no configurations exist
                                                                                    for the module we want just use default here (for example /api) . This should ideally come as part of middleware but I've implemented it in this way  //9   and then load modules dynamically with a specific path provided by url.pathname or from config file on each request
    if (!modulePath ) modulePath = '/default';                   
                                                                                        (for example /api)          . This should ideally come as part of middleware but I've implemented it in this way  //10   and then load modules dynamically with a specific path provided by url.pathname or from config file on each request     '^',(starts after ^ - only accept HTTP POST data
                                                                                            if not accepted, send back an error message via JSON as default (for example /api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way  //12   on any request that is sent to our application or after all requests have been served
    try {                                                             //     accept HTTP POST data via JSON , if not accepted, send back an error message - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way  //13   on any request that is sent to our application or after all requests have been served
        const reqBody = await Deno.readTextFile('body');      //     accept HTTP POST data via JSON , if not accepted, send back an error message - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way  /^
        console.log(reqBody);                                   //16             If a body was provided then print out to log file , if not accept POST data, send back error message - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way  /^
    } catch (error){                                               //17             If an exception was thrown then print out to log file , if not accept POST data, send back error message - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way  /^
        console.log(error);                                    //18             If an exception was thrown then print out to log file , if not accept POST data, send back error message - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way  /^
    } else {                                                      //20            Else return a JSON response with status code '418', you can add any custom data here. You will also receive HTTP POST body as json object from next request - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way  /^
        return new Response('Hello, API!');                      //21            Else return a JSON response with status code '408', you can add any custom data here. You will also receive HTTP POST body as json object from next request - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way  /^
    }                                                                 //23            Log error to console, if a specific route does not exist return response with status '405'. you can add any custom data here. If no POST body is provided then log an appropriate message - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
    try {                                                             //26            Receive HTTP Post Body as JSON, parse into object if not accepted send error response with status '418'. You can add any custom data here - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
        const parsedBody = await Deno.readTextFile('body');      //27            Receive HTTP Post Body as JSON, parse into object if not accepted send error response with status '418'. You can add any custom data here - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
        console.log(parsedBody);                                   //30            If a body was provided then print out to log file, if not accept POST request with status '418'. You can add any custom data here - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
    } catch (error){                                               //32            If an exception was thrown then print out to log file, if not accept POST request with status '418'. You can add any custom data here - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
        console.log(error);                                    //35            If an exception was thrown then print out to log file, if not accept POST request with status '418'. You can add any custom data here - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
    } else {                                                      //37            Else return a JSON response with code 20, you may also send http post body as json object from next request if available - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
        const data = {message: 'Hello API!', customData: {} };    //38            Else return a JSON response with code 20, you may also send http post body as json object from next request if available - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
        console.log(data);                                   //41            Log data to log file or return response with JSON, you may also send http post body as json object from next request if available - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
    }                                                                  /^         On all requests receive HTTP POST data via form-data or multipart/form, add to log file. If not provided then send back an error message - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
    try {                                                             //43            Receive HTTP Post Body as JSON from request , parse into object if POST body is accepted, handle response accordingly. If not provided then send error message - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
        const parsedBody = await Deno.readTextFile('body');      //45            Receive HTTP Post Body as JSON from request , parse into object if POST body is accepted, handle response accordingly If not provided then send error message - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
        console.log(parsedBody);                                   //50            Log parsed JSON to log file or return HTTP POST body as json object from next request if available, else send error message - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
    } catch (error){                                               //47             If an exception was thrown then log to console or return HTTP POST body as json object from next request if available, else send error message - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
        try {                              , (incorrect characters after )!  //48            If an exception was thrown then log to console or return HTTP POST body as json object from next request if available, else send error message - default (/api)          .         const parsedBody = await Deno.readTextFile('body');      //71            If an exception was thrown then log to console or return error response with status '408'. You may add any custom data as per requirement - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
        } catch (error){                           ,(incorrect characters after )!  //73            If an exception was thrown then log to console or return error response with status '408'. You may add any custom data as per requirement - default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
    }                                                                     /^         Log errors catch block for every request, if not found then return response with status '405'. });  //76 &8   If no POST body was provided or accepted HTTP Post Data, then return error message. log to console with specific route name by user-provided pathname if available else default (/api)          . This should ideally come at the end of your function and I've implemented it in this way
